Delicious apples. Tree looks old and ravaged, with few branches, but excellent apples. Falling Fruit season: September-September.
Dwarf gala apple tree. On the hill behind the 4 separate community beds that belong to Beardsley.
Between Broadway Carpet's fence and the road.
This house has an apple tree in the front yard that drops apples on the sidewalk and in the yard. The owners don't seem to pick the apples.
Overhanging the entrance to the alley Access: Private but overhanging.
Falling Fruit access: Private but overhanging.
Raspberries or blackberries (can't remember which) planted in the fall of 2010.
1579 Andy Holt Ave, Knoxville, TN 37916, USA
Many blueberry bushes along the pedestrian walkway. Access: Public. Falling Fruit season: June-unknown.
Raspberries and blueberries planted by the Tour de Plants riders in the now "closed" Fort Kid playground. Plants generously provided by The Tomato Head.
On the UT campus, behind the John XXIII Catholic center there is a large fig tree. Falling Fruit season: September-October.

Nice sized fig tree loaded with figs. When I visited in the first week of Oct only one fig was purple, the rest were green but the purple fig was delicious. Right at the corner of Luttrell and 3rd in the green space between the side walk and the road. Access:
Fig tree behind the Glowing Body. Belongs to and is tended by them. Ask permission before harvesting. They let me take a cutting to root. Access: Private.
Fig tree and other edibles in the garden in front. Access: Private.
Growing near the stoop of an empty lot. Lots of fruit for the whole neighborhood and a million june bugs. Exact location not precise, but it's right on the edge of the property.
Newly planted: will need several years before it bears nuts.
On North Broadway just north of Wells, in front of the First Lutheran Church and School sign. (a couple of blocks north of the Three Rivers Co-op, same side of street.)

walk off of the greenway path and you should see a fairly young pecan tree on the treeline, on the west side of first creek. There is a second tree down the same treeline just north of E. Glenwood, but I'm a little fuzzy as to where the property line of the ne
Young pecan tree starting to drop fruit. It's not very mature at this point, so it'll be one to watch in the coming years.
Several pawpaws growing in protective boxes. They are very young and will probably not bear fruit for several years.

Lovely peach tree in someone's fenced backyard. Always ask permission before entering someone's fenced yard to collect fruit. Access: Private.

Large, round, yellow fruit. Behind the middle brick building.
Large yellow pears. Grows in someone's yard, so ask permission before entering his property. Access: Private.
Small oblong fruit. Smells like a pear and has pear-like speckling
fruit of the hood...the property that the tree is on is vacant although you might want to ask the neighbors.
Tall tree on the edge of the mowed area. Orange-ish purple fruit drops in fall. Is powerfully bitter before ripening.

Row of wild plum trees with excellent harvest late summer. Part of the Borderland Tees Butterfly Garden. Garden is primarily for wildlife but includes human edibles too. Check with owners at Borderland Tees before gathering except for small amounts. Access: Ad

Large, mature serviceberry in on private property in the front yard. I'm happy to share-- if you can beat the birds to them. Please ask first to get permission. Access: Added by owner. Falling Fruit season: May-unknown.

I wouldn't harvest these as they grow so close to I-40 and James White Parkway, but if you want to see an example, there are several here. The drupes are best harvested in early fall...from a site less likely to be polluted by engine exhaust.
